characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The former of the title brothers. Cat is the smarter and more sophisticated of the pair, often coming up with hare-brained get-rich-quick schemes and being literally dragged around by his dimwit brother. Abhorrent Admirer: He is occasionally portrayed being attracted to Shriek, who, like the other Greasers, despises cats. Ax-Crazy: In "Monster Truck Folly" he ends up snapping and attacking everyone unprovoked. Beware the Silly Ones: While not particularly silly, Cat is humorous through his constant misfortune, snark, and snobbishness. As "Monster Truck Folly" shows, when he gets pushed too far, he can fly into anUnstoppable Rage. Big Brother Bully:Depending on the Writer, he may be one to Dog by abusing him, yelling at him, or manipulating him for his own personal gain.

characters.name: Cat
characters.description: The former of the title brothers. Cat is the smarter and more sophisticated of the pair, often coming up with hare-brained get-rich-quick schemes and being literally dragged around by his dimwit brother. Abhorrent Admirer: He is occasionally portrayed being attracted to Shriek, who, like the other Greasers, despises cats. Ax-Crazy: In "Monster Truck Folly" he ends up snapping and attacking everyone unprovoked. Beware the Silly Ones: While not particularly silly, Cat is humorous through his constant misfortune, snark, and snobbishness. As "Monster Truck Folly" shows, when he gets pushed too far, he can fly into anUnstoppable Rage. Big Brother Bully:Depending on the Writer, he may be one to Dog by abusing him, yelling at him, or manipulating him for his own personal gain.

characters.name: Dog
characters.description: The other end of the eponymous conjoined brothers. Dog is a happy-go-lucky dimwit who loves food and chasing vehicles, often to the frustration of Cat. Annoying Younger Sibling: To his older twin brother Cat, albeit usually unintentionally. Berserk Button: Stealing his bones from his bone cellar is really not a good idea. As well as marking your territory on his hydrant. Beware the Nice Ones: Cat once learned the hard way not toactuallymake Dog angry. Big Brother Instinct: Inverted; he's very, very protective of his older twin brother, Cat.

characters.name: Winslow
characters.description: A blue, Brooklyn-accented mouse who lives in the walls ofCatDog's home. He's usually on pretty good terms with his neighbours, but has been known to take enjoyment in tormenting them from time to time. Abusive Parents: His main purpose in life is to makeCatDogmiserable. In "The Great Parent Mystery" it's revealed that he found them as babies and raised them. Black Bead Eyes: One of the few characters with this eye style. Cartoon Creature: Despite being referred to as a mouse, he doesn't really look like one. Catchphrase: "What are you, nuts!?"Lampshaded in "CatDogCandy""I'm not even asking anymore, You guys are nuts!"

characters.name: Mervis and Dunglap
characters.description: A pig and a weasel who are amongCatDog's few friends. Dark Horse Victory:WhenCatDograces Mindy to the top of an unnamed mountain, Dunglap somehow made it up first since Cat forgot his earmuffs. Informed Species: Dunglap looks more like some kind of rodent than a weasel. Messy Pig: Mervis, for a given definition of "messy". Nice Guys: They're among the few characters, along with Lola, who treatCatDognicely.

characters.name: Mr. Sunshine
characters.description: A green-skinned humanoid of unclear species usually seen working various odd jobs. Despite his name, he is anything but sunshine. Ambiguously Human: On the one hand, he's far more humanoid-looking than most of the other characters; on the other, he has green skin and a tail. Bystander Syndrome: He'd probably be theOnly Sane Manif he didn't succumb to this all the time. Cartoon Creature: Nobody's quite surewhathe is. One episode even lampshaded the ambiguity of his species by showing that Rancid Rabbit had no idea what he was supposed to be.Word of Godsays he was originally supposed to be a monkey. Deadpan Snarker: Sometimes.

characters.name: Lola Caricola
characters.description: A "yellow-bellied whippoorwill" with a Hispanic accent. She's a zoologist who first showed up to studyCatDog, but soon became one of their closest friends. Action Girl: She gained her love of zoology after helping her family wrangle cattle. Not to mention the fact that she can go toe to toe withthe Greasers. Artistic License – Ornithology: She looks and acts nothing like the type of bird she's supposed to be. Badass Bookworm: She's a capable zoologist who can throw down in a fight when necessary. In fact, she first became interested in zoology from helping her family wrangle cattle. Catchphrase: "Unbelievable!"

characters.name: Randolph Grant
characters.description: A suave and trendy feline and a local celebrity in Nearburg. Ambiguously Gay: Overall the mannerisms of this celeb and posh stylish character show hints of being gay and at times he seems to have a thing for Dog, especially in episodes "CatDog's End", "Dog the Not so Mighty", and "Dog Ate It". Catchphrase: "And I LOVE it!" Dodgy Toupee: It is revealed in "Picture This" that his hair isn't real. Kent Brockman News: He's Nearburg's newscaster

characters.name: The Ingrid Twins
characters.description: A pair of female cats who have datedCatDoga few times. Always Identical Twins: They look alike and even have the same name. Opposites Attract: In one episode, it's shown that theGenki GirlIngrid likesDeadpan SnarkerCat and theDeadpan SnarkerIngrid likesGenki GuyDog. Sexy Scandinavian: Both are beautiful blondes and presumably Swedish, sharing a stereotypical Scandinavian name. Ship Tease: They often appear on dates withCatDog.

characters.name: The Greasers
characters.description: The archenemies ofCatDog. A trio of thuggish dogs who hate cats and beat up any they meet. The gang consists of the brutish leader Cliff; the astoundingly moronic Lube; and the diminutive Shriek, who is the sole girl of the group. Big, Thin, Short Trio: Cliff is the big one, Lube is the skinny one, while Shriek is the short one. Black Bead Eyes: Like Winslow, Shriek has these. Lube had these as well, but in the pilot "Dog Gone",as well as more hair combed at the front. Co-Dragons: They sometimes help out Rancid Rabbit, when he's the main antagonist of the episode. A Day in the Limelight:Cliff is the main focus of "Squirrel Dog" (where he must learn to accept life as a "freak" after Eddie the Squirrel is accidentally glued to him) and "Cliff's Little Secret (whereCatDogblackmail him with an embarrassing secretnoteHe takes up ballet.)Shriek has "The Lady is a Shriek", which focuses on her trying to become more lady-like for Dog.Lube has "All You Need is Lube" (where he becomesCatDog's servant as a thanks for Cat rescuing him from a tick) and "Lube in Love" (where he falls in love with a beautiful woman and enlistsCatDog'shelp to woo her).As a whole, they are the focus of "Rich Shriek, Poor Shriek", where their relationship is strained due to the difference in classes, and "Greasers in the Mist", where Lola tries to find their weakness.

characters.name: Rancid Rabbit
characters.description: A large green rabbit who antagonizesCatDogjust as often as the Greasers do. Somewhere between the mayor and the CEO of Nearburg, he seems to have managerial-level positions in virtually every business in the city. Big Bad: Nearly every episode he's in has him as the main antagonist. Big Bad Wannabe: Deteriorates into this as the show went on. Black Bead Eyes: He has small dots for eyes like several other characters. Catchphrase: "Leaping lumbago!" and "What what WHAT!?"

characters.name: Eddie the Squirrel
characters.description: A mangy little squirrel with a bad attitude who idolizes the Greasers and seeks to join their gang. Ambiguously Gay: He was more than content to be stuck with Cliff forever the moment he was glued to his jacket. The Dog Bites Back: In "New Cat in Town" he gets back at the greasers for all the ridicule they gave him by building a giant robot akin to a movie they watched called "Catzilla". He later enslavesCatDogand the rest of Nearburg as well. Harmless Villain: Of all the people who actively antagonizeCatDog, he's the most ineffectual and isn't really a threat. Ineffectual Sympathetic Villain: Heavy on the ineffectual, light on the sympathetic.

characters.name: Tallulah Headbank
characters.description: A feline actress whom Cat has an enormous crush on. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: A gorgeous movie star who is also vain and snobbish. Cats Are Mean: She's an unpleasant cat. Celeb Crush: Cat has one on her. Jerkass: Noticing a pattern here?

characters.name: "The Mailman"
characters.description: A fast-moving lizard who works in postal delivery. Bald of Evil: When seen without his hat on, he is shown to be bald. Big Bad: Of "Fistful of Mail". Formerly Fat:As the Paperboy, he was so chubby as to be unrecognizable as a slender, sleek adult. An interesting example, as its only relevance to the plot is to reveal just why Dog didn't recognize him at first. Jerkass: He picked on Dog ever since he was a puppy.

characters.name: CatDog'sparents
characters.description: CatDog's (adoptive) parents, whom the pair set out to find inThe Movie"CatDogand the Great Parent Mystery". Bigfoot, Sasquatch, and Yeti:Their mom appears to be some kind of sasquatch-creature—we never find out what she is and not even her husband seems to know. Catchphrase: Dog got his from his and Cat's mom. Deadpan Snarker:CatDog's dad is one, which is where Cat gets it from—seeing and hearing their father make the same sarcastic remark as him is what ultimately leads Cat to accept that these two are indeed his and Dog's parents. Genki Girl: Dog gets his cheerful personality and energetic demeanor from his and Cat's mom—the two bond almost instantly.
